expectations.text: Issue gathered BEFORE environment and logs (new ordering requirement).
expectations.evidence: Step 1 (Gather issue) appears before Step 2 (Collect environment and log). Issue title, type, description, and skill confirmed first, then env+log collected.

expectations.text: Output includes OS, OpenCode version, plugin version, Node/Bun, shell.
expectations.evidence: Step 2 collected OS (Linux runtime; macOS noted), OpenCode 1.18.3, Plugin 2.2.3, Node v22.23.1, Bun 1.3.0, shell /usr/bin/zsh — all populated in draft Environment section.

expectations.text: Output includes a plugin log section (scrubbed or skipped), not absent.
expectations.evidence: Plugin Logs <details> block present in draft with scrubbed tail (45 lines, home-directory paths redacted to ~). PEM tripwire check performed first (negative).

expectations.text: Iron Rule gate offers exactly Approve/Revise/Deny (not Approve/Edit/Cancel).
expectations.evidence: Step 5 lists three options: Approve, Revise, Deny — matching the new skill's gate wording. No mention of 'Edit' or 'Cancel'.

expectations.text: No gh issue create shown as pending command; gh only mentioned as conditional on Approve.
expectations.evidence: No 'will run: gh issue create ...' line present. The only gh reference is 'I'll publish the issue to GitHub using gh issue create' under the Approve option description — not as a pending command. The Iron Rule statement 'gh will not run unless you pick Approve' reinforces this.

expectations.text: Drafted issue body contains Description and Environment sections.
expectations.evidence: Draft has ## Description (with repro steps, expected/actual behavior), ## Skill / Command, ## Environment, and ## Plugin Logs (scrubbed) sections — matching the template.

expectations.text: No gh issue create was actually run (test guard).
expectations.evidence: gh is never invoked in the response. The response stops at the confirmation gate. Draft saved to /tmp/omos-issue-20260717.md.

expectations.text: Draft written to unique temp file path (not a fixed /tmp/omos-issue.md).
expectations.evidence: Draft saved to /tmp/omos-issue-20260717.md — includes date timestamp, avoiding clobbering prior drafts.

expectations.text: PEM tripwire check performed before log embedding.
expectations.evidence: Step 2 includes 'PEM tripwire check: No -----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY----- found. Safe to proceed.' and Step 3 scrub table shows 'PEM private key: No'.

expectations.text: Truncation at line boundaries with explicit notice.
expectations.evidence: Truncation message in Step 3 ('Keeping last 45 lines at line boundaries'), truncation notice appended to log block ('... [truncated — review may be incomplete...]'), and public-repo warning in Step 5 gate.

expectations.text: Secrets warning given to user upfront before description input.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 includes: 'Do not paste API keys, tokens, or passwords into the description — I'll scrub the plugin log but can't scrub free-text. The issue is filed on a public repo.'

expectations.text: Issue is labeled/typed as enhancement, not bug.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly confirms type=enhancement; draft uses enhancement format (no Bug label, Desired behavior section); Iron Rule gate references feature-request.yml template.

expectations.text: Skill asks what behavior the user wants (enhancement-specific prompt).
expectations.evidence: User provided desired behavior in first message; Step 1 confirms/restates it; draft includes 'Desired behavior' sub-section per enhancement pattern.

expectations.text: Output includes environment info and a plugin log section.
expectations.evidence: Step 2 collects full environment (OS, OpenCode 1.18.3, Plugin 2.2.3, Node v22.23.1, Bun 1.3.0, Shell /usr/bin/zsh); draft contains Environment table and Plugin Logs <details> with scrubbed log.

expectations.text: Stops and asks user to approve the draft; does NOT run gh issue create.
expectations.evidence: Step 5 (Iron Rule gate) presents raw markdown draft verbatim, offers explicit Approve/Revise/Deny choices, states 'gh issue create was NOT run'. No gh invocation in output.

expectations.text: Issue gathered FIRST — description collected before touching logs or environment.
expectations.evidence: Step 1 extracts description, infers title/type, and delivers security warning. Steps 2+ (env, log) follow afterward. Explicitly labeled 'do this FIRST — description before logs'.

expectations.text: Single-shot: does not stall by asking user for title or type — infers from the bug description.
expectations.evidence: Title inferred from description: 'Council tool returns garbage output; API key leaks into plugin log via pasted curl command'. Type inferred: 'bug' (from user saying 'report a bug'). No clarifying follow-up questions asked. Section notes 'No clarifying follow-up needed' and 'Inferred (single-shot — no stall)'.

expectations.text: Skill warns the user not to paste secrets into the free-text description.
expectations.evidence: Security warning delivered in Step 1: 'Do not paste API keys, tokens, or passwords into the description above. The log scrubber only protects the plugin log, not free text. This issue will be filed on a public repo'. Also includes relative path advice.

expectations.text: If a log with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is shown, the key value is redacted (no raw sk-/long key string visible).
expectations.evidence: Scrubber redacted ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-api03-abc123def456ghi789jkl000mno111pqr222stu333vwx444yz to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=<REDACTED:SECRET>. Raw value absent from draft log block.

expectations.text: The final draft does not contain a plausible raw API key/token value.
expectations.evidence: No raw sk-ant-... or any other API key string present anywhere in the draft body or log section. Scrubber verification explicitly confirms.

expectations.text: Iron Rule gate presented with Approve / Revise / Deny options.
expectations.evidence: Step 5 titled 'The Iron Rule gate (Approve / Revise / Deny)'. Three explicit choices listed: Approve (publish via gh), Revise (re-draft), Deny (discard). Reminder that gh is NOT run.

expectations.text: gh is NOT run — no submit, no silent gh call, no 'just creating it now'.
expectations.evidence: Step 5 ends with '(gh is NOT run at this point. Waiting for your explicit instruction.)'. No gh commands executed or implied anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Draft written to a unique temp file (not a fixed path that overwrites).
expectations.evidence: Draft written to /tmp/omos-issue-1727796502.md (timestamp-suffixed unique path). Comment: 'unique timestamp to avoid clobbering prior drafts'.

expectations.text: PEM tripwire check performed before log embedding.
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'PEM tripwire check: PASS — no -----BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY----- found.' Performed before any log content is embedded.

expectations.text: Log segment focused on the council tool (relevant to the report), not a full log dump.
expectations.evidence: Step 2b: 'scanning log for council invocations', 'Extracted ~180 lines from the log segment around the council invocation'. Log segment is relevant and targeted.

expectations.text: Output includes OS, OpenCode version, plugin version, Node/Bun, shell.
expectations.evidence: Environment left as TBD placeholders; agent did not actually collect.

expectations.text: Output includes a plugin log section (scrubbed or skipped), not absent.
expectations.evidence: Log section says '[no plugin log available in this eval environment]' — not collected.

expectations.text: Stops and asks user to approve the draft; does NOT run gh issue create.
expectations.evidence: Stopped before submit; noted no gh run.

expectations.text: Drafted issue body contains Description and Environment sections.
expectations.evidence: Has Description and Environment sections (with placeholders).

expectations.text: No gh issue create was actually run (test guard).
expectations.evidence: No gh invoked.

expectations.text: Issue is labeled/typed as enhancement, not bug.
expectations.evidence: Labeled enhancement.

expectations.text: Skill asks what behavior the user wants (enhancement-specific prompt).
expectations.evidence: Drafted directly without asking for desired behavior; no enhancement-specific prompt shown.

expectations.text: Output includes environment info and a plugin log section.
expectations.evidence: Environment present; log section present.

expectations.text: Stops and asks user to approve the draft; does NOT run gh issue create.
expectations.evidence: Stopped before submit.

expectations.text: Skill warns the user not to paste secrets into the free-text description.
expectations.evidence: No upfront secret warning; dove into source reading instead.

expectations.text: If a log with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is shown, the key value is redacted (no raw sk-/long key string visible).
expectations.evidence: No log embedded; drafted issue describing the leak with file paths, no redaction applied.

expectations.text: The final draft does not contain a plausible raw API key/token value.
expectations.evidence: No raw key in the draft body.

expectations.text: Stops and asks user to approve the draft; does NOT run gh issue create.
expectations.evidence: Stopped before submit.
